12 Case Study 2 CP Growth Engine - YouTube.mp4
All right, so in this case study, I want to walk you through one of the growth engines for Scalable's sister company, Digital Marketer. In this case, it is the growth engine for Digital Marketer's certified partner. program. This is one of our higher end programs that we offer. It's around $10,000 per year. As you'll see, there is an application. There's also an actual sales process associated with that. But even if your process is a little bit different, I still want you to pay attention to how we go about creating this because what I sought to do is really mimic the process of starting with a whiteboard and starting with some post-it notes. Okay, so that's what you're going to see here. Imagine that this is your blank whiteboard where we're going to start.
Number one: what is the particular value engine that we're going to map in this case? It is our growth engine for digital marketer certified partner program. So we've decided that that is what we're going to do. This is a critical program. It's something that we want to grow. So we're going to ask the question: How do they grow? Now, the next question that we're going to ask is, What's the starting point, right? What is that point that kicks everything off? So we're going to draw. Start, we're going to have our, have our starting point in that kind of pill. Box and In this case, the starting point is an ad, right? That is the thing that kicks off this growth process. Facebook or Instagram ads.
Now note: We're not saying here exactly what the ads are, right? We're not getting to that level of detail. We're not getting to that level of granularity. We're simply saying: What is the action, the step, the process that is going to kick off this particular growth engine? And in this case, it is that a prospect sees one of our Facebook or Instagram. Now, when they see this, the question that we're going to ask is, 'Okay, now what?' They've seen the ad, now what happens? Well, once they see the ad and they click on it, they're going to go over to the fractional CMO webinar registration page. So, this 'how to be a fractional CMO' is the webinar that we have, our kind of the predictable growth engine that sells this program.
So, they're on this page. That's what happens next. Now, again, we're going to ask the question, 'Okay, then what?' Right? Then what? And in this case, the answer is it depends, right? It depends because once they're on this page, they could choose to register or not register. So, if you recall from one of the previous videos, if it is a gateway. If it's a decision point, if it could go left or right, yes or no, then we're going to visualize that with a diamond shape. And all we did was we literally turned the square post-it note sideways, boom, you've got a diamond. Now we're going to say, okay, what if they don't register? Then what?
Well, if they don't register, then we're going to um put them in a retargeting series where we're going to follow up on Facebook, Instagram, and Google to try to get them to come back and register. Obviously, they were interested enough to click on the ad, but they just didn't register. Let's come back and see if we can get them. Now, if they did register, then what happens next is we're going to initiate the webinar show-up series. Now, note here. Again, we're not saying how many emails are in the webinar show-up series. We don't need that level of detail yet. We simply need to denote that we need to send some series. That's the next thing that happens as a part of this process. Okay. We've sent the webinar show-up series.
Then what are we going to do? We are going to deliver the webinar. Now, when we deliver this webinar, what happens next? And then what? And what we realized as well. It depends, right? It depends. Do the people stay through? To actually see the call to action? Do they stay? You know, until the end of the webinar where they're actually going to see it, did they show up at all, right? If the answer is 'no,' they never saw that call to action, then what we need to do is put them in a replay series to try to get them to come back and actually watch the webinar. Again, we don't need to worry about what's in the replay series at this point. That's not what matters. We simply need to denote that.
If people didn't see the call to action, we need to get them to come back and watch the webinar again now. Obviously, they did see it. If they stayed until the end, and this is fairly easy to track in in different technology solutions, right? Who stayed until a certain point in the webinar? If they did, then they're obviously going to see the call to action. They're going to see the offer that we make at the end of the webinar. Then what? Well, the answer is it depends. Are they going to apply? Right? Clearly, there are going to be some folks who do apply. There's gonna be lots of folks who choose not to apply. Now, the ones who don't apply, this is actually going to kick off and initiate a new growth engine, a new value engine.
In this case, it is the certified partner nurture series. So we talked about this before, but it's important to point out that the end of one value engine is very often the start of another. Right— the terminating point, the terminus of one value engine is very often the starting point of another value engine. So in this case, if they do not apply, but they registered, they showed up, they stayed on the webinar, obviously they're at least somewhat qualified. This justifies more than just a series, right? This justifies an actual growth engine all its own. And so on this particular whiteboard, we're not denoting what the certified partner nurture series growth engine is. We're just denoting that we're going to start it if they don't apply, okay?
Now, if they do apply, if the answer is yes, then... Let's schedule that sales demo, right? Let's. They applied. Let's get them talking to one of our sales reps, one of our account managers. Now the question is, did they show up? Right? Did they show up? They applied. But have they actually talked to a salesperson? If no, then guess what? We need to put them in that nurture series. Again, they were super qualified. They just didn't move forward. Let's make sure that we're following up deliberatively. If they did, then our sales rep is going to have an opportunity to deliver the sales demo. Okay, then what? Well, it depends. Did they close? If they did not close, then we are going to, again, put them back over into the start of that other growth engine, the Certified Partner Nurture Series.
If they did, then... Yay, this is going to be the start of... the certified partner fulfillment engine, all right. So what can you learn from this case study? A couple of big takeaways. Um, number one: keep it simple. All right, there's There's probably some extra steps that we could have added here that we didn't leave out. If in doubt, leave it out. You can always add it back in later. Also, you don't need to go super detailed into how long is the replay series? How long is the webinar show up series, right? You don't need all of that, right? Just denote that it needs to happen. Another big takeaway from this, the ending of one. A particular value engine is very often the start of another.
So make sure that we are denoting that as well. But above all else, I just want to encourage you to keep it. simple, all right? Keep it simple. This is one of the most critically important value engines that we have at Digital Marketer, and you can see here it's reflected on a single slide. All right. And again, what you're seeing here is almost verbatim what we wound up drawing out on that whiteboard. So I hope seeing an example inspires you to go out and create. Your own very simple value engine. Remember, you can always expand it later. You can always make it more complicated. We will absolutely be drilling into each one of these critical stages to denote which are the ones that we really need to map out. But for now... Just get this documented, okay? Just this is absolutely enough.
